title: Final Capstone: Neighborhood Resource Guide History
summary: Build a complete local history for a neighborhood resource guide while keeping changes focused, generated artifacts ignored, and the final repository easy to hand off.

sketchBlocks.title: Welcome to the Final Capstone
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
In this final capstone, you will apply your Git skills to create a local history for a neighborhood resource guide. Your task is to ensure that the guide is well-maintained, with clear commit history and ignored generated artifacts. This project will help future volunteers understand changes, recover from mistakes, and trust the final handoff.

### Project Brief



**Scenario:** A neighborhood help desk is replacing a folder of loose text files with a maintained resource guide. Future volunteers must be able to see when service information changed, recover from small mistakes, and trust the final handoff.

**Your role:** Junior developer preparing the neighborhood resource guide for long-term maintenance

**Workspace:** One cumulative terminal workspace rooted at resource-guide/ with pages/, notes/, generated/, README.md, and no starting repository

**Deliverable:** One six-step local Git capstone with four meaningful commits, ignored generated previews, a tracked file rename, a safe obsolete-file removal, required final content, and a clean handoff state.

quizDraft.title: Create the Guide Baseline
quizDraft.prompt:
Turn the existing `resource-guide/` folder into a Git repository on `main`, configure the repository-only learner identity, stage the initial guide files, and commit `Start neighborhood resource guide`.

First type `bash .zoeskoul/setup.sh` and press Enter. Then enter `resource-guide/`, type each Git command, and press Enter after every command.
quizDraft.hint: Use the lesson explanation and the wording of this question to narrow the answer.

help.concept: This support text was repaired because the original wording revealed the answer too directly.
help.hint_1: Compare the question to the lesson example and remove details that do not match the requested concept.
help.hint_2: Use the role or evidence named in the prompt instead of relying on answer wording.

quizDraft.starterCode:
# First type: bash .zoeskoul/setup.sh
# Press Enter, then follow the capstone step in the prompt.
quizDraft.solutionCode:
bash .zoeskoul/setup.sh
cd resource-guide
git init -b main
git config --local user.name "ZoeSkoul Learner"
git config --local user.email "learner@zoeskoul.local"
git add README.md pages/contact.md pages/guide-draft.md notes/old-phone-tree.txt
git commit -m "Start neighborhood resource guide"
quizDraft.instructions:
Turn the existing `resource-guide/` folder into a Git repository on `main`, configure the repository-only learner identity, stage the initial guide files, and commit `Start neighborhood resource guide`.

First type `bash .zoeskoul/setup.sh` and press Enter. Then enter `resource-guide/`, type each Git command, and press Enter after every command.

starterFiles.content:
# First type: bash .zoeskoul/setup.sh
# Press Enter, then follow the capstone step in the prompt.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu
rm -rf -- "resource-guide"
mkdir -p -- "resource-guide/pages" "resource-guide/notes" "resource-guide/generated"
printf '%s\n' '# Neighborhood Resource Guide' '' 'Welcome to the neighborhood resource guide.' > "resource-guide/README.md"
printf '%s\n' '# Contact Information' '' 'Help Desk Hours: 9 AM - 4 PM, Monday to Friday' > "resource-guide/pages/contact.md"
printf '%s\n' '# Guide Draft' '' 'Use this page to find neighborhood help.' > "resource-guide/pages/guide-draft.md"
printf '%s\n' 'Legacy volunteer phone tree' > "resource-guide/notes/old-phone-tree.txt"

solutionFiles.content:
bash .zoeskoul/setup.sh
cd resource-guide
git init -b main
git config --local user.name "ZoeSkoul Learner"
git config --local user.email "learner@zoeskoul.local"
git add README.md pages/contact.md pages/guide-draft.md notes/old-phone-tree.txt
git commit -m "Start neighborhood resource guide"

printf '%s\n' '# Guide Draft' '' 'Use this page to find neighborhood help.' > "resource-guide/pages/guide-draft.md"
printf '%s\n' 'Legacy volunteer phone tree' > "resource-guide/notes/old-phone-tree.txt"

quizDraft.title: Add the Services Page
quizDraft.prompt:
Continue the same guide by creating `pages/services.md` with the authored local-services list, stage only that file, and commit `Add local services page`.

First type `bash .zoeskoul/setup.sh` and press Enter to restore the completed baseline. Then enter `resource-guide/`, create the page, run the Git commands, and press Enter after each command.

starterFiles.content:
# First type: bash .zoeskoul/setup.sh
# Press Enter, then follow the capstone step in the prompt.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu
rm -rf -- "resource-guide"
mkdir -p -- "resource-guide/pages" "resource-guide/notes" "resource-guide/generated"
printf '%s\n' '# Neighborhood Resource Guide' '' 'Welcome to the neighborhood resource guide.' > "resource-guide/README.md"
printf '%s\n' '# Contact Information' '' 'Help Desk Hours: 9 AM - 4 PM, Monday to Friday' > "resource-guide/pages/contact.md"
printf '%s\n' '# Guide Draft' '' 'Use this page to find neighborhood help.' > "resource-guide/pages/guide-draft.md"
printf '%s\n' 'Legacy volunteer phone tree' > "resource-guide/notes/old-phone-tree.txt"
git -C "resource-guide" init -q -b main
git -C "resource-guide" config user.name "ZoeSkoul Learner"
git -C "resource-guide" config user.email "learner@zoeskoul.local"
git -C "resource-guide" add README.md pages/contact.md pages/guide-draft.md notes/old-phone-tree.txt
git -C "resource-guide" commit -q -m "Start neighborhood resource guide"

cd resource-guide
printf '%s\n' '# Local Services' '' '- Community pantry' '- Senior transport' > pages/services.md
git add pages/services.md
git commit -m "Add local services page"

quizDraft.title: Publish Updated Help Desk Hours
quizDraft.prompt:
Change `pages/contact.md` so the help desk closes at 5 PM, inspect the unstaged diff, then commit `Update help desk hours`.

First type `bash .zoeskoul/setup.sh` and press Enter to restore the first two commits. Enter `resource-guide/`, update the page, type `git diff` and press Enter, then stage and commit the change.

quizDraft.title: Ignore Generated Preview Files
quizDraft.prompt:
Create `.gitignore` with the rule `generated/`, stage `.gitignore`, and verify that `generated/preview.html` is ignored. Do not commit yet; this staged rule belongs with the final cleanup commit.

First type `bash .zoeskoul/setup.sh` and press Enter to restore the three committed milestones. Enter `resource-guide/`, type each command, and press Enter after every command.
quizDraft.hint: Read the Git task "Ignore Generated Preview Files" and identify the repository state you need to create.

help.concept: This Git exercise checks the repository state produced by "Ignore Generated Preview Files".
help.hint_1: Use git status before and after each small change so you can see what moved between the working tree, staging area, and history.
help.hint_2: Match the requested file names, branch names, and commit purpose before clicking Check Answer.

quizDraft.starterCode:
# First type: bash .zoeskoul/setup.sh
# Press Enter, then follow the capstone step in the prompt.
quizDraft.solutionCode:
bash .zoeskoul/setup.sh
cd resource-guide
printf '%s\n' 'generated/' > .gitignore
git add .gitignore
git check-ignore generated/preview.html
quizDraft.instructions:
Create `.gitignore` with the rule `generated/`, stage `.gitignore`, and verify that `generated/preview.html` is ignored. Do not commit yet; this staged rule belongs with the final cleanup commit.

First type `bash .zoeskoul/setup.sh` and press Enter to restore the three committed milestones. Enter `resource-guide/`, type each command, and press Enter after every command.

quizDraft.title: Rename the Guide and Remove the Obsolete Note
quizDraft.prompt:
Use Git-aware file operations to rename `pages/guide-draft.md` to `pages/getting-help.md` and remove `notes/old-phone-tree.txt`. Keep these staged changes together with the staged `.gitignore`; do not commit yet.

First type `bash .zoeskoul/setup.sh` and press Enter to restore the staged ignore rule. Enter `resource-guide/`, type `git mv` and press Enter, then type `git rm` and press Enter.
quizDraft.hint: Read the Git task "Rename the Guide and Remove the Obsolete Note" and identify the repository state you need to create.

help.concept: This Git exercise checks the repository state produced by "Rename the Guide and Remove the Obsolete Note".
help.hint_1: Use git status before and after each small change so you can see what moved between the working tree, staging area, and history.
help.hint_2: Match the requested file names, branch names, and commit purpose before clicking Check Answer.

quizDraft.starterCode:
# First type: bash .zoeskoul/setup.sh
# Press Enter, then follow the capstone step in the prompt.
quizDraft.solutionCode:
bash .zoeskoul/setup.sh
cd resource-guide
git mv pages/guide-draft.md pages/getting-help.md
git rm notes/old-phone-tree.txt
quizDraft.instructions:

quizDraft.title: Commit and Verify the Final Handoff
quizDraft.prompt:
Commit the staged ignore rule, tracked rename, and obsolete-note removal with `Prepare resource guide handoff`. Then inspect the four-commit history and confirm the working tree is clean.

set -eu
rm -rf -- "resource-guide"
mkdir -p -- "resource-guide/pages" "resource-guide/notes" "resource-guide/generated"
printf '%s\n' '# Neighborhood Resource Guide' '' 'Welcome to the neighborhood resource guide.' > "resource-guide/README.md"
printf '%s\n' '# Contact Information' '' 'Help Desk Hours: 9 AM - 4 PM, Monday to Friday' > "resource-guide/pages/contact.md"
printf '%s\n' '# Guide Draft' '' 'Use this page to find neighborhood help.' > "resource-guide/pages/guide-draft.md"
printf '%s\n' 'Legacy volunteer phone tree' > "resource-guide/notes/old-phone-tree.txt"
git -C "resource-guide" init -q -b main
git -C "resource-guide" config user.name "ZoeSkoul Learner"
git -C "resource-guide" config user.email "learner@zoeskoul.local"
git -C "resource-guide" add README.md pages/contact.md pages/guide-draft.md notes/old-phone-tree.txt
git -C "resource-guide" commit -q -m "Start neighborhood resource guide"
printf '%s\n' '# Local Services' '' '- Community pantry' '- Senior transport' > "resource-guide/pages/services.md"
git -C "resource-guide" add pages/services.md
git -C "resource-guide" commit -q -m "Add local services page"
printf '%s\n' '# Contact Information' '' 'Help Desk Hours: 9 AM - 5 PM, Monday to Friday' > "resource-guide/pages/contact.md"
git -C "resource-guide" add pages/contact.md
git -C "resource-guide" commit -q -m "Update help desk hours"
printf '%s\n' '<html>generated preview</html>' > "resource-guide/generated/preview.html"
printf '%s\n' 'generated/' > "resource-guide/.gitignore"
git -C "resource-guide" add .gitignore
git -C "resource-guide" mv pages/guide-draft.md pages/getting-help.md
git -C "resource-guide" rm -q notes/old-phone-tree.txt

forbiddenCommands.message: Do not rewrite shared history in this Git practice task.
